channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: Russia has grappled with a shrinking and aging population for decades, with President Vladimir Putin viewing it as a threat to national security.
item.dc:creator: Katie Marie Davies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa poised to become a hurricane and drop torrential rain on Caribbean
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa is expected to strengthen into a hurricane, threatening the northern Caribbean with massive rainfall and life-threatening flooding.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump’s Asia return and East Timor’s entry take spotlight in landmark ASEAN summit
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — Southeast Asian foreign ministers opened talks Saturday ahead of a landmark ASEAN summit that will formally welcome East Timor as the bloc’s 11th member and mark U.S. President Donald Trump’s first trip to Asia since returning to the White House.
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's new leader faces diplomatic gauntlet with Trump, China and regional summits
item.description: Japan's new Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi faces significant foreign policy challenges just days after taking office.
item.dc:creator: Ken Moritsugu And Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 48 bags of suspected human remains found at Mexican gravesite
item.description: Previous discoveries of bags found buried in Jalisco state gravesites have included bone fragments and human tissue.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Houthi rebels detain 2 more UN staffers in Yemen as the world body reevaluates its operations
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Houthi rebels detained two additional United Nations staff members and raided the homes of several others in Yemen over the last 48 hours, the latest in a series of events now forcing the world body to reassess how it operates in the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and AHMED AL-HAJ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rubio tours US-led center in Israel overseeing the ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: KIRYAT GAT, Israel (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday toured a U.S.-led center in Israel overseeing the Gaza ceasefire, as the Trump administration worked to set up an international security force in the territory and shore up the tenuous truce between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US deploys Ford carrier strike group to combat narco-terror in Western Hemisphere
item.description: The Gerald R. Ford Carrier Strike Group is being deployed to continue targeting suspected drug smuggling boats in the Caribbean, the Pentagon said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's GOP allies look to seize Trump's momentum versus Putin
item.description: Senators are hoping to seize the momentum as President Trump goes on the offensive against Russian President Vladimir Putin, though Republicans remain cautious about getting ahead of the commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar in vivid hat, long hidden from view, sells for 32 million euros
item.description: A long-hidden Picasso portrait of muse Dora Maar has sold for 32 million euros at an auction in Paris.
item.dc:creator: Oleg Cetinic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US kills 6 suspected narco-terrorists in overnight strike on alleged drug smuggling boat, Hegseth says
item.description: War Secretary Pete Hegseth announced the tenth strike on an alleged drug smuggling boat, saying another six alleged narco-terrorists were killed overnight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Canada 'trying to illegally influence' Supreme Court on tariffs
item.description: President Trump defended his decision to call off trade negotiations with Canada on Friday, accusing the U.S.'s northern neighbor of attempting to "illegally influence" a pending Supreme Court case regarding tariffs. "CANADA CHEATED AND GOT CAUGHT!!!" Trump posted to Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mancini, The Hill

item.title: Reporter’s Notebook: A Paris jewel heist straight out of the movies
item.description: A movie-style heist at the Louvre saw criminals scale the museum with a cherry picker truck and steal priceless French crown jewelry before fleeing on scooters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: International Criminal Court judges reject jurisdiction challenge in Duterte case
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — International Criminal Court judges on Thursday rejected a challenge to jurisdiction in the case of former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte, who is accused of involvement in dozens of killings as part of his so-called war on drugs when in office.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy urges US to broaden Russian oil sanctions and seeks long-range missiles
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has urged the United States to expand sanctions on Russia’s two biggest oil companies to the whole sector.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa stationary in the Caribbean as 4 deaths reported and huge rains expected
item.description: Tropical Storm Melissa is nearly stationary in the central Caribbean, with forecasters warning it could soon strengthen and brush past Jamaica as a powerful hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Krafton shifting to 'AI-first' game development
item.description: The makers of popular video game "PUBG: Battlegrounds" are switching gears and becoming an "AI-first" developer.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Tropical Storm Melissa lumbers through the Caribbean, killing an elderly man in Haiti
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — Tropical Storm Melissa lumbered through the Caribbean Sea on Thursday, bringing a risk of dangerous landslides and life-threatening flooding to Jamaica and southern Hispaniola — an island shared by the Dominican Republic and Haiti.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump cuts off trade talks with Canada over Reagan tariff ad
item.description: President Trump said Thursday he was cutting off trade negotiations with Canada, citing an ad campaign that used former President Reagan’s warnings about the long-term risks of tariffs amid the trade dispute between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Putin deplores US sanctions as 'unfriendly' while EU joins in heaping restrictions on Russia
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The European Union on Thursday heaped more economic sanctions on Russia, adding to U.S. President Donald Trump’s new punitive measures the previous day against the Russian oil industry.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bipartisan senators criticize Trump’s nominee for Kuwait over accusations of antisemitism
item.description: Republican and Democratic members of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee on Thursday demonstrated rare unity opposing President Trump’s nominee for ambassador to Kuwait, scrutinizing a history of alleged antisemitic comments and positions.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians attend rival rallies in Budapest as Orbán faces election test
item.description: BUDAPEST, Hungary (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of Hungarians filled the streets of Budapest on Thursday in competing demonstrations as supporters of the country’s two main political movements staged mutual shows of strength before next spring’s national election.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Russia won't bow to the US
item.description: President Vladimir Putin cautioned that it would deliver an “overwhelming” response to any military strikes deep inside Russia.
item.dc:creator: By Ksenia Orlova and Maxim Rodionov, NewsNation

item.title: Putin adviser says Trump chose 'path of war' with oil sanctions
item.description: Former Russian President Dmitry Medvedev said Thursday the country no longer would have to worry about “meaningless” peace negotiations with President Trump, a day after the Treasury Department announced sanctions against two Russian oil companies.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: British ex-soldier acquitted of murder in 1972 Bloody Sunday massacre
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A former British paratrooper was found not guilty Thursday on murder charges relating to the 1972 Bloody Sunday massacre in Northern Ireland that saw 13 people killed in a flurry of bullets.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK police arrest 3 men in London on suspicion of spying for Russia
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British counterterrorism police arrested three men in London on Thursday on suspicion of spying for Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American missionary kidnapped in Niger by suspected Islamist militants, sources say
item.description: Suspected Islamist militants have kidnapped an American missionary in the West African nation of Niger, U.S. official sources told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel 'not a protectorate' of the US, Netanyahu says ahead of meeting with Vance
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel's prime minister toughened his stance Wednesday by declaring that his country is in charge of its own security and isn't an American protectorate as he prepared to discuss progress on Gaza’s fragile ceasefire agreement with U.S. Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Vance meets with Netanyahu, urges patience as Gaza ceasefire holds
item.description: After meeting with Israel's prime minister, Vance said the United States' role was to support and safeguard the ceasefire, not to "monitor a toddler.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Massive Russian attack on Ukraine kills at least 6, officials say
item.description: Ukraine’s energy minister said a “massive combined overnight attack” on Ukraine’s energy infrastructure was still underway early Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan’s first female prime minister, citing Thatcher inspiration
item.description: Sanae Takaichi becomes Japan's first female prime minister, citing Margaret Thatcher as inspiration while promising to restore confidence and strengthen security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine and EU leaders accuse Putin of stalling, reject land concessions for peace
item.description: They accused Russian President Vladimir Putin of stalling for time in diplomatic efforts to bring his invasion of Ukraine to an end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy calls Trump meeting 'positive' though he didn't get Tomahawk missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says his reportedly tense meeting with U.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope meets with global group of clergy abuse survivors to talk zero tolerance
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV met Monday for the first time with an organization of clergy abuse survivors and advocates, who said he agreed to maintain a permanent dialogue as they press for a zero-tolerance policy for abuse in the Catholic Church.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump suggests US will buy Argentinian beef to bring down prices
item.description: “We would buy some beef from Argentina,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One during a flight from Florida to Washington.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel resumes ceasefire in Gaza and says aid deliveries will restart Monday
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Gaza's fragile ceasefire faced its first major test Sunday as Israeli forces launched a wave of deadly strikes, saying Hamas militants had killed two soldiers, and an Israeli security official said the transfer of aid into the territory was halted.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump says Ukraine's Donbas region will have to be 'cut up' to end the Russian invasion
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that the Donbas region of Ukraine should be “cut up," leaving most of it in Russian hands, to end a war that has dragged on for nearly four years. “Let it be cut the way it is,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, WGN 9
